exemplar.title: To what extent participation in Islamic activities such as Usrah and Quran recital can reduce stress and anxiety in Mara Colleges.

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent participation in Islamic activities such as Usrah and Quran recital can reduce stress and anxiety in Mara Colleges.

strengths: The research question is clearly stated and linked to a specific population and context.
Methodology is coherently explained, with appropriate use of Likert-scale survey and correlation analysis.
Topic background and objectives are well communicated, with relevant institutional and national data to ground the investigation.
weaknesses: Sampling strategy and instrument-validity checks are not fully described, limiting methodological rigor.
The research question phrasing (âcan reduceâ) implies causation and remains somewhat broad without specifying exact measures of stress/anxiety.
Operational links between each objective and specific, measurable indicators could be made more explicit.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates a generally appropriate quantitative methodology, communicates the topic clearly, and has a focused research question, but some detailsâparticularly around sampling, validity, and tightening of focusâremain under-developed. This performance corresponds to a solid mid-band score of 4 out of 6.

strengths: Demonstrates good understanding of Islamic concepts (Usrah, Qurâanic recital) and basic psychological constructs (stress, anxiety).
Terminology such as âLikert scaleâ and âcorrelation coefficientâ is generally used correctly and defined.
A wide range of sources is cited, including academic, governmental, and Qurâanic materials.
weaknesses: Use of source material is sometimes formulaic and relies occasionally on non-academic sites.
Glossary omits key methodological terms, reducing clarity for the reader.
Subject-specific terminology is accurate but not employed with consistent rigor or depth of theoretical insight.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the student shows good subject-knowledge and integrates a broad set of sources, the application is at times descriptive and the use of terminology uneven. Overall this maps cleanly to a band-middle performance of 3 out of 6.

strengths: Data analysis includes descriptive statistics, clear charts, and correlation coefficients linked back to each research objective.
Discussion connects findings to existing literature and acknowledges key limitations (e.g. sample size, programme intensity).
Argumentation follows a logical structure from introduction through analysis to conclusions, with evidence usually supporting claims.
weaknesses: Analysis remains confined to bivariate correlations with little interrogation of confounding variables, causal inference or statistical assumptions.
Evaluation of methodological weaknesses and alternative interpretations is relatively brief and could be deepened.
Some conclusions are stated more strongly than the data justify, leading to minor inconsistencies in the critical argument.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the studentâs critical thinking is stronger than the analysis alone might suggestâdemonstrating clear relevance of the data to the question and a well-structured argumentâthe depth of evaluation and critical interrogation is uneven. On balance, the work justifies a higher weighting in Critical Thinking given the coherent use of evidence, placing it in the lower range of the 7â9 band and awarding 8 out of 12.

strengths: The report has a clear title page, table of contents, and logically numbered sections that guide the reader effectively.
Headings and sub-headings are used consistently, supporting comprehension of the line of reasoning.
Figures and tables are present and labelled, and the overall layout is coherent and professional.
weaknesses: Citation style and in-text referencing are somewhat inconsistent.
Minor formatting errors persist (e.g. typo in âTable of Contents,â inconsistent subsection heading styles).
Some charts would benefit from direct data labels and clearer axis titles to improve standalone readability.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Despite a handful of typographical and styling inconsistencies, the structure and layout of the investigation fully support reader understanding. Presentation merits the top band of 3â4, and given the overall coherence and readability of the document, a score of 4 out of 4 is justified.

strandSelections.reasoning: The argument follows a logical structure from introduction through data analysis to conclusion, and claims are usually linked to evidence.  Nevertheless, sections tend to be descriptive, and some assertions (e.g., causative effects of participation) are stated more strongly than the data justify, leading to minor inconsistencies.  Hence the argument is clear but not yet finely honed.

A wide range of contemporary academic, governmental and Qurâanic sources is cited and a full bibliography is provided.  Source material is usually integrated into the narrative, but in-text referencing is sometimes formulaic and some claims rely on popular websites.  Application of sources in the analysis is adequate but not consistently critical, placing the work comfortably in the âmostly relevant/adequate applicationâ band.

The student outlines an anonymous survey using Likert scales, explains the construction of composite scores, and applies correlation tests; this represents a generally appropriate quantitative approach for the question.  A literature review is also used to give conceptual context.  However, details about sampling strategy, instrument validity and data-analysis procedures beyond simple correlation are limited, so the methodology is âmostly completeâ rather than fully comprehensive.

The title, background and objectives clearly identify the twin foci of Islamic practices (Usrah and Qurâan recital) and student mental-health outcomes.  The introductory section explains why these variables matter, gives relevant national and institutional statistics and links them convincingly to the context of MARA Colleges.  Minor lapses in expression and some repetition mean the communication is not consistently precise, but overall the reader understands what is being investigated and why.

A word count, title and section headings are provided; charts and tables are labelled; and the bibliography follows a recognisable format.  Page numbers are referenced in the table of contents but not visible in the excerpt, and citation style is somewhat inconsistent, so layout requirements are met but with minor imperfections.

The student provides descriptive statistics, visual charts and correlation values to address each objective, showing a logical link to the research question.  However, analysis is confined to simple bivariate correlations with limited critical interrogation of causality, confounding variables or statistical assumptions.  This constitutes adequate but only partially developed analysis.

Key technical terms such as âcorrelation coefficientâ, âLikert scaleâ, âtaâarufâ, âtakafulâ and psychological categories are used correctly and usually defined.  Occasional spelling inconsistencies (e.g., âmildnessâ) and informal phrasing reduce precision, so the use of terminology is mostly accurate rather than consistently rigorous.

The discussion summarises key findings, relates them to cited literature and notes sample-size limitations and programme-intensity as possible influences.  Conclusions are generally supported by the presented data, yet evaluation of methodological weaknesses and alternative interpretations is brief.  Overall, the discussion is good with some well-supported conclusions but not consistently rigorous.

The essay contains a title page, table of contents, logically numbered sections, and a clear progression that makes it easy to follow the line of reasoning.  Headings and sub-headings are used effectively and support reader understanding, fulfilling the criteria for a clearly appropriate structure.

The research question is explicitly stated and refers to a specific population, practices and outcomes, which provides a workable investigative focus.  Nevertheless, the wording (âto what extentâ¦can reduce stress and anxiety in MARA Collegesâ) remains somewhat broad because it does not specify which dimensions of stress/anxiety or the precise indicators of participation that will be measured, so the focus is not fully tight.  Hence the question is clear but only partially focused.

The essay demonstrates solid understanding of both mental-health constructs (stress, anxiety) and the Islamic concepts of Usrah and Qurâan recital, supported by current statistics and Islamic scholarship.  Explanations are generally accurate, though occasionally descriptive and lacking theoretical depth, indicating good rather than outstanding subject knowledge.

annotations.comment: Figure 3 would benefit from direct data labels on bars and axis titles to enhance readability without consulting the legend or table.
Table 3 shows mean participation scores but omits measures of dispersion (e.g. standard deviation); include these to reveal withinâgroup variability.
The narrative uses speculative language (âvery well may followâ); align interpretations closely with data and avoid conjecture without evidence.
The heading reads âTABLE OF CONTENTâ rather than âTABLE OF CONTENTS.â Correcting this typo will improve professionalism and consistency.
The statistic â1 in every 7 adolescents experiencesâ¦ stressedâ needs a precise WHO report reference (title, year, page) to ensure source verifiability and academic rigour.
Several entries in the table of contents lack page numbers, reducing navigational clarity; ensure all section headings are matched with accurate page references.
While the hadith citation is relevant, include translation, context and citation details to support readers unfamiliar with Islamic scripture.
The methodology section does not describe the sampling strategy or instrument validity checks; include details on sample selection, response rate and reliability measures to strengthen the design.
The title is clearly stated at the start of the report, meeting the requirement for the investigation title to be shown prominently (Formatting).
The word count is provided and falls within the 4 000-word limit, satisfying the formatting requirement (Formatting).

The research question implies causation (âcan reduceâ) and remains broad; it would benefit from specifying which dimensions of stress/anxiety and the exact participation measures to tighten focus and reflect the correlational design.
Redundant phrasing ânowadays are nowâ weakens readability; streamline to âTeenagers today faceâ¦â for clearer expression of the context.
The discussion of Islamic practices introduces valuable psychological mechanisms (community, mindfulness), showing good relevance to the research question.
Table 2 notes average anxiety but does not discuss potential contextual factors beyond program intensity; exploring support services or cultural influences would enrich discussion.
The description of survey distribution across four MARA colleges clearly defines the sample frame and links back to research objectives.
Percentages of respondents by college (24â26.2%) lack raw counts; including n values will allow critical appraisal of sample distribution.
Your use of the WHO definition provides a solid conceptual foundation, demonstrating clear subject knowledge and understanding of mental health constructs.
While the objectives are listed clearly, linking each to specific, measurable indicators (e.g. stress scale thresholds) would enhance focus and operational clarity.
The phrase âThe most common mental health for teenagersâ is ambiguous; specify whether you mean symptoms, disorders or prevalence to improve precision.
Your discussion of Usrahâs psychological benefits demonstrates good critical linkage between religious practices and mental-health theory.
The claim that adding Islamic practices will reduce stress cites a 2025 source but lacks critical evaluation; caution against overgeneralising effects without supporting current empirical data.
The anxiety definition would benefit from reference to DSM-5 criteria or validated clinical descriptions to align with psychological standards.
The NIMH statistic on adolescent anxiety (31.6%) should specify the survey year, population and methodology for full contextual relevance.
Your justification for focusing on Usrah and Quran recitation is well-argued, showing clear alignment between chosen methods and research objectives.
Figure 2 lacks comparative benchmarks; consider including normative anxiety levels for similar populations to contextualise findings.
Background on Usrah relies heavily on secondary literature; including primary qualitative data (e.g. participant quotes) would strengthen the critical evaluation.
Section heading formatting for â5.2 Level of participationâ¦â is inconsistent with other subsection headers; use uniform styles for cohesion.
Use proper LaTeX syntax for equations (e.g. $$r = -0.549$$) rather than <math> tags to maintain consistency and readability of statistical expressions.
The claim that âmost respondents reported feeling calmerâ should be linked to specific survey items and percentages to substantiate this assertion with data evidence.
Objective numbering in the Data Analysis repeats earlier Objective 5.0.1â5.0.3; revise numbering for consistency and avoid reader confusion.
Figure 1 presents stress level distribution clearly; the visual aid supports the narrative effectively by matching chart and table data.
The conclusion restates findings well but would benefit from deeper reflection on study limitations (e.g. sample representativeness) and targeted future research suggestions.
Treating Likertâscale responses as interval assumes equal spacing; discuss whether ordinal data can validly underpin Pearson correlations or consider non-parametric alternatives.
Table 1 lists average stress scores but omits the scale maximum (e.g. out of 50); stating the possible range would improve interpretability.
Table 4 presents correlations but lacks degrees of freedom or test statistics; include these details for complete reporting of significance tests.
The glossary defines Islamic terms well but omits key research terms (e.g. âLikert scaleâ, âcorrelation coefficientâ); including these will aid reader comprehension of methodology.
Bibliography entries exhibit inconsistent citation style and ordering; adopt a uniform format (e.g. APA) and alphabetise to meet formatting standards.
